test_cases.input: Draft article about a school principal accused of bullying staff. Mix of on-record quotes, anonymous claims, and characterizations. Principal is a private individual.
test_cases.notes: Correctly applied higher risk standard for private individual. Anonymous bullying claims correctly flagged Red. Attribution for a Green passage was appropriately confirmed as protected.

test_cases.input: Profile of a city councilmember (public figure) with allegations of financial conflicts related to a development vote. Mix of documented public records and one unsourced allegation.
test_cases.notes: Correctly applied lower threshold for public figure. Unsourced allegation was still Red-flagged even under actual malice standard. Documented public record passages correctly assigned Green.

test_cases.input: Investigative feature about a named corporation (not individual) with allegations of wage theft from employees. UK publication.
test_cases.notes: Correctly switched to UK libel framework when jurisdiction was specified. Noted that UK law places burden of proof on publisher, not plaintiff — a meaningful difference in risk assessment. One passage categorization could have been Amber rather than Red.

editorial_naturalness_baseline.protocol: EVAL_FRAMEWORK.md Section A — Editorial Naturalness rubric (1-5 anchored on observable lexical, structural, tonal, and genre tells).
editorial_naturalness_baseline.calibration_methodology: Single-output baseline pass scoring the in-skill canonical example (the dimension reviewer's primary calibration target per the AUT-23 SKILL_TEMPLATE.md update).
editorial_naturalness_baseline.tells_observed: None. Latinate verbs that appear ('substantiate,' 'corroborate,' 'adjudicate') are legal terms of art, correctly used. 'Significantly' appears in legal-register technical context ('substantially more plaintiff-friendly').
editorial_naturalness_baseline.strongest_genre_craft: The Red/Amber/Green tiering with per-passage Legal Concern + Recommended Action + Suggested Rewrite is the standard structure of an in-house counsel pre-publication note. The closing disclaimer ('This brief is an editorial pre-screening tool. It is not a substitute for review by a qualified media attorney.') is the kind of italicised note a real media lawyer would write.
editorial_naturalness_baseline.note_on_pre_existing_fields: The overall_mean, dimension_means, and per-test-case scores below are preserved as a record of the original eval session and were never updated by the AUT-7 actionability relift. The eval_score in the skill frontmatter and EVAL_SUMMARY.md table reflect post-AUT-7 + post-AUT-23 values; this baseline block is the new authoritative source for those numbers.
